rulings.Case Title: YOU LIANG SONG VS. ARTHUR LIU ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Enter Judgment/Request For Entry Of Formal Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the LAW AND MOTION / DISCOVERY Calendar for Wednesday, Apr-29-2026. LINE 10. PLAINTIFF YOU SONG's Motion To Enter Judgment/Request For Entry Of Formal Judgment. Per order of the Presiding Judge, the motion is transferred to be heard in Department 302 in front of Judge Quinn on May 20, 2026. Judge Van Aken is recused. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: AMIR GUPTA VS. LEAH CULVER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Response To Form Interrogatories - Construction Litigation, Set One; Numbers 305.13, 309.2, 314.4 And 326.1; And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the LAW AND MOTION / DISCOVERY Calendar for Wednesday, Apr-29-2026. LINE 2. PLAINTIFFS AMIR GUPTA, AN INDIVIDUAL, and PRAVANA CAPITAL PARTNERS, INC.'s Motion To Compel 1-Further Response To Form Interrogatories - Construction Litigation, Set One; Numbers 305.13, 309.2, 314.4 And 326.1; And Request For Sanctions. Amir Gupta and Pravana Capital Partners Inc.'s ("Defendants") motion to compel further responses to Construction Litigation Form Interrogatories, Set One, Nos. 305.13, 309.2, 314.4, and 326.1, and for sanctions, is granted in part. ***THE COURT'S COMPLETE TENTATIVE RULING HAS BEEN EMAILED TO THE PARTIES*** For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES TED HARVEY VS. ANDREW REBENNACK ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Determination Of Attorney-Client Privilege
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the LAW AND MOTION / DISCOVERY Calendar for Wednesday, Apr-29-2026. LINE 3. PLAINTIFF JAMES HARVEY's Motion For Determination Of Attorney-Client Privilege. Plaintiff James Ted Harvey's motion to determine whether the attorney-client privilege exists as to certain communications withheld by defendants Andrew Rebennack, Kathy Maionchi, Daniel Maionchi, Clark Maionchi, and Dominic Maionchi, and to compel production of those documents, is denied. The court concludes both parties' discovery positions had substantial justification and does not award sanctions. ***THE COURT'S COMPLETE TENTATIVE RULING HAS BEEN EMAILED TO THE PARTIES*** For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. rulings.Case Title: PETER ALLEN VS. WEDRIVEU HOLDINGS, INC., ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Joint Notice Of Settlement And Motion For An Order For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement [C.C.P. 877.6(A)(2)]
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the LAW AND MOTION / DISCOVERY Calendar for Wednesday, Apr-29-2026. LINE 4. DEFENDANTS WEDRIVEU, INC. (ERRONEOUSLY SUED HEREIN AS WEDRIVEU HOLDINGS, INC.), and CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S Joint Notice Of Settlement And Motion For An Order For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement [C.C.P. 877.6(A)(2)]. Defendants WeDriveU, Inc. and the City and County of San Francisco's opposed motion for a determination of good-faith settlement pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure, Sec. 877.6, is granted. The court has considered the factors set out in Tech-Bilt, Inc. v. Woodward Clyde Associates (1985) 38 Cal.3d 488, 499, including a rough approximation of the plaintiff's total recovery, a rough approximation of the settling defendant's proportionate liability, the amount paid in settlement, and any evidence of collusion or fraud. The court concludes that the settlement reached by the settling defendants is proportionate to their potential liability at trial such that it is in " 'the ballpark' " (id., at pp. 499-500) of the settling defendants' potential liability in light of their showing that Phoenix has admitted that it was the owner and operator of the bus that discharged Plaintiff into traffic. Even assuming the truth of Phoenix's facts that the City allows commuter shuttles to park at the location where Plaintiff exited the Phoenix bus, or that a City bus struck Plaintiff with its mirror as he exited, the contribution by WeDriveU and the City to an overall assumed $300,000 case value is not so small as to be outside of the ballpark of a good-faith settlement. Moreover, the City and WeDriveU present evidence of their nonliability, including Plaintiff's deposition transcript that his knowledge of a City bus's involvement in the accident is based on hearsay. (Egler Reply Decl., Ex. F, pp. 19-20.) In addition, there is no evidence of any collusion or fraud. The court concludes that the settlement is in good faith. This finding bars all existing, potential, and future claims by, through and against WeDriveU and the City for equitable comparative contribution, or partial or comparative indemnity, based upon comparative negligence or comparative fault. The City's cross-complaint is dismissed. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)
